thread.title: Missoula homicide trial vacated as defense withdraws
thread.title_full: Missoula homicide trial vacated as defense withdraws

author: Kylie Gibson
title: Missoula homicide trial vacated as defense withdraws
text:
MISSOULA, Mont — A Missoula homicide case set for trial in December has been vacated.
Miles Otis Miller was set to stand trial in the deliberate homicide charge against him.
Miller allegedly fatally stabbed his mother on May 13, 2023, in the home they shared in the 500 block of South Avenue.
NBC Montana attended today's open court hearing at the Missoula County Courthouse where Miller appeared in person.
Due to a conflict that was brought up this week in the case, the defense requested a motion to withdraw from the case.
Judge Leslie Halligan granted the motion and vacated the trial, with no objections from the state.
Halligan asked Miller if he was ok with the defense withdrawing.
“They are requesting based on their review of conflicts and their ethical obligation that they be permitted to withdraw and that new council will be found for you, are you in agreement with that?” asked Halligan.
“Umm, I mean, I like my current council but I do understand that they have ethical obligations,” Miller stated.
Miller has pleaded not guilty to the charges of deliberate homicide and tampering with or fabricating evidence.
A status hearing will take place on Dec. 18 to discuss how the case will move forward.
If found guilty, Miller could spend the rest of his life behind bars.
